key_insight: The paper introduces a fully differentiable, matrix-free PyTorch phase-field fracture solver that runs on CPUs/GPUs and supports both forward simulation and inverse parameter identification.
engineering_relevance: It enables scalable fracture analysis and gradient-based calibration without custom compiled extensions, making fracture simulation easier to integrate with optimization and machine-learning workflows.
method: Element-wise finite-element tensor contractions with scatter-based accumulation replace global sparse stiffness assembly in explicit dynamics.
Supports AT1/AT2 regularizations, multiple energy splits, and plane strain/plane stress options.
Uses autograd for explicit mechanics and a custom implicit-differentiation backward rule for the bound-constrained damage solve via CG.
evidence_signal: The solver was benchmarked on four dynamic and two quasi-static fracture cases and recovered fracture energy Gc to below 10^-3 relative error in inverse analysis.
watch_item: Performance and robustness may depend on CG convergence and GPU memory/throughput when scaling to very large meshes or tightly constrained inverse problems.
paper_title: A matrix-free, differentiable PyTorch solver for phase-field fracture: Formulation, benchmarks, and inverse analysis
